title: Meeting Rooms
description:
## Meeting Rooms

Given an array of meeting time intervals where `intervals[i] = [start_i, end_i]`, determine if a person could attend all meetings — i.e., no two meetings overlap.

Two meetings overlap if one starts before the other ends (touching end-to-start is allowed).

**Constraints:**
- `0 <= intervals.length <= 10^4`
- `intervals[i].length == 2`
- `0 <= start_i < end_i <= 10^6`

solutions.label: Sort and Check — O(n log n)
solutions.explanation: Sort meetings by start time. Then check each consecutive pair: if the next meeting starts before the current one ends, there's an overlap and we return False. Touching (start of next == end of current) is fine.

solutions.label: Brute Force — O(n²)
solutions.explanation: Check every pair of intervals for overlap. Two intervals [a_start, a_end] and [b_start, b_end] overlap if a_start < b_end AND b_start < a_end (they truly intersect, not just touch). O(n²) time since we check all pairs — much slower than the O(n log n) sort approach.

hints: Sort by start time. Then check whether any consecutive meetings overlap.
Two meetings overlap if the next one's start time is strictly less than the previous one's end time.
